close_reason: keyring::Entry::set_password is atomic at the OS level; cancel-safety is inherent. No code change needed.
description:
Trigger: caller drops the put() future (timeout, select!, abort) before the spawn_blocking task completes.
Behavior: tokio::task::spawn_blocking is detached from the JoinHandle on cancel; the blocking thread continues running and may successfully call entry.set_password(). The caller's perspective: the put() future was cancelled, so they assume no write happened. Reality: the write happened.

Impact: silent inconsistency between caller-perceived state and keychain state. Subtle bug in retry logic — caller retries with a different value, may overwrite a successful write with the second one (still consistent), or may abort and leave a write the caller intended to discard.

Fix: document that put() is not cancel-safe in the doc comment for WritableSecretStore impl. No code change needed if the contract is documented.

Same pattern likely in secretx-keyring and secretx-systemd; mention as cross-crate.
title: put() is not cancel-safe; partial writes possible if future is dropped
